description:
In the heart of the downtown, a short drive to the beach, with a dedicated parking space, this is the perfect place for enjoying Wilmington's night life and the ocean.

The space
Unit J is a spacious, stylish, fully-furnished 1-bedroom loft in the heart of historic downtown Wilmington.

A gorgeous urban space surrounded by history. This is the master suite -- the largest unit in the building -- of the Pontiac Place building, which was a Pontiac dealership and service center in the 40s and 50s and was converted to condos in the 90s. The building has a rich history and boasts the only underground parking garage downtown. You will very likely park your car in the cool, climate-controlled garage, designed to house brand-new Pontiacs, and walk to everything you want to do.

In 2018, I pulled Unit J off of Airbnb and moved into it. I remodeled -- new floors and kitchen -- and it is better than ever. I now live nearby, and it is again available for rent.

Though it is steps to dozens of bars and restaurants, Unit J is also private and quiet, secreted in the center of the block where you will hear almost none of the downtown festivities.

Unit J is roomy, with 1200 square feet of living space, a full dining area, a living room, a huge bedroom, and two bathrooms. The exposed brick walls soar to meet the 22-foot ceilings with exposed beams and girders. The floors are all new, and the kitchen has been recently remodeled. The upstairs bathroom has a shower, bath, and bidet. The downstairs bathroom has a shower stall and a bidet.

If you are traveling for an event or with friends, our other unit -- The Eye of Pontiac [airbnb.com/h/eyeofpontiac] -- is directly across the hall. Rent them both and use Unit J to gather, sip cocktails, and eat a meal.

The unit is next to Cape Fear Community College, two blocks from the Cape Fear River. Surrounded by Wilmington's finest restaurants and nightclubs, Pontiac Place is an easy walk to the Live Oak Bank Pavilion at Riverfront concert venue, the Brooklyn Arts District, several wedding venues and hotels, the downtown convention center, the Cape Fear River Walk, and the water taxi to the USS North Carolina battleship. It's a quick 7-minute drive from Wilmington International Airport (ILM) and a short drive to more than a dozen attractions, including world-class golf courses and Wrightsville Beach.

Stroll two blocks to the Riverwalk and enjoy the incredible views of the Cape Fear River. Walk to a Live Nation concert. (We provide chairs if your tickets are for the lawn.) Take a horse-drawn carriage through one of the largest and prettiest historical districts in the country, home to both Revolutionary and Civil War heroes. Take a water taxi across the Cape Fear to tour the USS North Carolina, a decommissioned WWII-era battleship. Drive out to Wrightsville Beach (25 mins) and enjoy 85-degree water and 10 miles of pristine sand; you can surf, parasail, or jet ski. Take a sail along the Intracoastal Waterway or kayak through gorgeous, unspoiled marshland. Dive WWII-era wrecks and sunken artificial reefs within 45 minutes of shore. There are half a dozen great golf courses and amusement areas within a short drive as well. You won't be bored.

The open-plan downstairs living space features a couch, several comfy armchairs, plenty of reading material, a view of Front Street, a 54-inch smart HDTV, cable, and blazing-fast WiFi.

A dining table easily seats four and can be expanded to host a larger group.

There is a roomy desk with a keyboard tray and lighting to make working as you travel comfortable.

The fully equipped kitchen includes a refrigerator, induction range, dishwasher, microwave, Keurig coffee maker, toaster, full complement of plates, cutlery, and utensils. The open shelves are stocked with enough fine glassware to host a glamorous cocktail party.

The loft bedroom features a deliciously comfortable memory foam King-sized bed and a huge and comfy memory foam bean bag. The bean bag is so big you can -- and will -- sleep on it.

Free WiFi Internet, Cable TV.

Coin-op laundry in the building.

Sleeps four, if the four of you are a couple sharing the bed, with one child on the bean bag and another on the couch. (An adult could sleep on either of those, too, of course.) The space is perfect for single travelers looking for a home on the road, couples, or families that have outgrown hotel rooms. Brides love Unit J because of the location, vast space for gathering, and the dedicated vanity area upstairs.

Longer-term rentals are available.

NO SMOKING. NO PETS.

Guest access
You can park briefly in the guest spots in the parking lot. There is a dedicated underground parking space, included, in the garage. There is also usually street parking. An inexpensive public garage is a block away.

Other things to note
It is a short walk--two blocks--to the Riverwalk, which lets you walk downtown along the river.
There is a flight of stairs in the unit, and you will need to climb one flight of stairs to get from the underground garage to the building.

description:
Experience the 'Ice Palace,' a luxury 3BR/2BA Italian-inspired artist's home in Wilmington. This unique modern 2600 sq ft space for 6 features a gourmet kitchen, marble floors throughout, cathedral ceilings, open spaces, wood-burning fireplace, and a large fenced yard with grill. Perfectly located near Wrightsville Beach and Downtown, it's an unforgettable creative retreat. High-speed Wi-Fi and premium amenities including bikes and beach gear. Message me with any questions.

The space
Welcome to the “Ice Palace,” a 2600 sq ft luxury Italian-inspired artist's home where modern design meets creative inspiration. With gleaming white marble floors throughout, this unique home offers an unforgettable stay. After a day of exploring, unwind in the stylish living room with a cozy wood-burning fireplace, or lounge in the art-filled gallery.

Our home features three beautifully appointed bedrooms:
* Primary “Lotus” Suite: A spacious King bedroom showcasing a large, hand-painted lotus mural with an en-suite bathroom featuring a walk-in shower and a bidet. Memory foam mattress, and lots of drawer space.
* Matisse Room: A serene Queen bedroom with a black out blind,
* Miro Room: A comfortable vibrant Queen bedroom with tv

The shared guest bathroom is a work of art itself, with floor-to-ceiling marble, Italian glass mosaic accents, and a granite vanity.

We provide everything you need for a comfortable and luxurious stay:
* Luxury 100% cotton linens, down duvet comforters, and a mix of down & memory foam pillows.
* Plush bathrobes, towels, and beach towels.
* Natural toiletries, including hand soap, body wash, shampoo, and conditioner.
* High-speed Fiber 2G Wi-Fi.
* We use Eco-Friendly All Natural Cleaning Products, a whole home de-chlorinator system, RO filtered drinking water.
* In-room televisions with streaming, DVDs, books, and board games.

Large backyard enclosed with a six-foot fence great for pups, we have a doggy door insert available to provide 24-hour access to the backyard for your pup!

The gourmet designer kitchen is a chef's dream, equipped with marble countertops, a gas cooktop, All-Clad cookware, an espresso machine, a French press, and countless cooking accessories. We use eco-friendly cleaning products and provide a whole-home de-chlorinator system and RO filtered drinking water.

Guest access
Guests will have private access to the entire home and its unique themed spaces. Enjoy your morning coffee in the screened-in \World Fusion room

description:
Enjoy everything Wilmington offers from it's award winning restaurants to it's top notch night life. Everything located within one block walking distance. You will be relaxing in a one bedroom apartment with full amenities that include wireless Internet , all linens , full kitchen and separate living room. Enjoy breath taking views from your balcony that over looks the Cape Fear river.

The space
-Secure building, elevator
-Washer/Dryer in unit
-All linens provided

Guest access
~The whole place is yours!

~1 Parking spot is provided off site in a secure parking garage 1.5 blocks away.
additional parking may be purchased for $15.00 per day.

Other things to note
~We are located on the coast and adverse weather is sometimes a factor, Please understand we DO NOT offer refunds for adverse weather conditions we will give you a credit for any monies paid to use for alternate dates within 60 days. We encourage you to purchase trip insurance through your local travel agent for such things as hurricanes and snow storms. Hurricane season is from June 1st thru November 30 please make a note of this when booking.

~We will supply a starter set of toilet paper, paper towels, a few dishwasher pods, small bars of hand soap beside sinks, and a few trash bags (kept under the current trash bag in the can). Please plan accordingly on what you need to bring depending on your length of stay.

~By booking this condo you agree to all the covid rules and restrictions put in place. If you have any questions concerning the rules and restrictions please message me before requesting to book. Booking constitutes acceptance of said covid rules and restrictions.

~Guest count must match reservation NO EXCEPTIONS!

~ DO NOT rent this property if you are not the guest staying. Must be 23 years old to rent this condo.

~QUIET times are observed between 9:00 pm and 8:00 am

~ NO PETS~

~Condo is set up for 2 sets of 2 guests sleeping in the same bed, if the pullout is needed please make sure to notify us 24 hrs before arrival for linens.

What's unique about The Blue Magnolia?
FRESHLY REDECORATED/PAINTED FOR 2024!
All private Guest Suites have private entrances (COVID safety), private porches, private ensuite baths, refrigerators with small freezers, microwaves, coffee makers, and flatscreen TVs....and a Welcome Basket for you to enjoy upon your arrival.
We do all that we can to provide a relaxing, enjoyable stay!
Registered Short Term Rental-City of Wilmington! (#STL2019-0029)

The space
Walk to everything- the Riverwalk and restaurants, downtown festivals, coffee shops, art galleries, clubs, and the lively Castle Antique District.

Rx Restaurant, Castle Street Kitchen, Jesters Restaurant, Luna Coffeeshop, Sanctuary Yoga, Gravity Records are all 2-4 blocks.
(** NEW Downtown Oyster Bar opening soon, Summer 2022, 1/2 block away, Castle and S 3rd Street!!)

We offer a beautiful, very private Guest Suite- bedroom with a Queen sized bed, private ensuite bath (shower and soaking tub), decorative fireplace, *private entrance* (COVID safety), and private porch with comfortable cahirs, fans, in a lovely garden setting.

Newly renovated 1870 downtown home with gleaming antique heart pine floors. The house recently won a Historic Preservation Award from the City of Wilmington (May 2013). You will get the feel of a classic 1870 Wilmington historic home, and you’re not going to lack what you need. We strive to use best local/organic/sustainable accessories and products.

\Green\" renovation elements include tankless hot water for unlimited hot water supply and insulated walls (interior and exterior) for comfort and quiet.

listing_name: Classy, Comfy Retreat 1 mi to Wrightsville Beach!
description:
Top-Rated 3BR with 300+ 5⭐ reviews”

1 mile to Wrightsville Beach— Walk to dining, Starbucks & shops. Upscale community w/ seasonal pool, tennis & pickleball. Great for families or couples! Enjoy easy access to crystal-clear beaches, surfing, boating, waterfront dining, sunset walks, and coastal nightlife. Ideal for vacation stays, remote work retreats, and family trips. Peaceful wooded neighborhood with mature oaks, landscaped grounds, and resort-style amenities for relaxation and recreation.

The space
Now for the good stuff! ✨

Nearby Attractions
Wrightsville Beach – 1.6-mile walk
Lumina Station – 3-minute walk
Airlie Gardens – 4-minute drive
Bluewater Waterfront Grill – 5-minute drive
Shell Island Wedding Venue – 9-minute drive
UNCW – 9-minute drive
Blockade Runner – 10-minute drive
Wrightsville Manor – 11-minute walk
Downtown Wilmington & Riverwalk – 7 miles

Step into a stylish, comfortable retreat designed for both relaxation and memorable gatherings.

🛌 Sleep in Comfort
Unwind in the upstairs master suite featuring a plush king-size bed and private balcony access—perfect for quiet mornings or evening wind-downs

Second upstairs bedroom offers a cozy queen bed for a restful night’s sleep

Downstairs bedroom with a queen bed and nearby full bath—ideal for guests who prefer main-level access

🚿 Spa-Like Bathrooms
Beautiful Italian marble finishes throughout
Refresh and recharge with a luxurious Moen rain shower experience

👨🏻‍🍳 Fully Equipped Kitchen
Cook with ease in a modern kitchen featuring granite countertops and custom cabinetry

Premium Samsung stainless steel chef’s refrigerator and full suite of appliances

Coffee maker and toaster oven included for quick breakfasts or late-night snacks

Open-concept design flows seamlessly into the dining and living areas—perfect for entertaining


😌 Relax & Unwind
Spacious living room with ultra-comfortable leather couch

📺 55” 4K Smart Roku TV with DVD player—log into all your favorite streaming apps
Samsung soundbar delivers an upgraded, theater-like experience
Fast, reliable WiFi throughout the home
Private Outdoor Escape
Step through elegant French doors onto your own secluded deck
Enjoy peaceful views with ample seating—perfect for morning coffee, sunset drinks, or simply unwinding

❤️ Thoughtful Extras
Hair dryer
Iron and ironing board

Guest access
Guests will have access to the whole residence. Full Access to the home, the pool is an outdoor community pool and it is NOT heated so its open seasonally, typically our HOA will open our pool in May. The pool key is kept on the key rack in the kitchen, its a large key on a cloth lanyard. Please remember to put the key back in the kitchen before you check out otherwise you will be charged. If your trip is strictly based on pool usage please ask us if it is open prior to booking. Washer and dryer for your convenience. Please do not put sandy items in the washer and dryer we ask that you shake the sand off outside first. Thanks

Other things to note
Guests must be 25 years old excluding minors with a parent. We ask that you please respect our home as if it was yours and most of all please respect our neighbors as our community has no loud noise after 10pm. Thank You!

description:
Centrally-located on the beautiful Cape Fear Coast, PalmTreeHouse is a charming, nautically-themed mid-century garage apartment, nestled among palm trees, and a private Zen Garden, with easy access to the Wilmington Riverfront, and the beaches.

As an extension of the automotive/industrially-themed PalmTreeHut downstairs apartment, you may book the PalmTreeHouse simultaneously for parties of four, or go solo on your Wilmington excursion.

The space
PalmTreeHouse is the name...and description...of this 700 square - foot garage apartment in the charming Chestnut Heights neighborhood of Wilmington, NC. With ample off-street parking, you will enjoy all the comforts of home as you explore the Cape Fear Coast, with fully furnished living room, kitchen, dining area, built - in workdesk, and bedroom with king bed. The PalmTreeHouse is the upstairs apartment of a two-unit building, with the industrially-themed PalmTreeHut downstairs.

From the spacious multi - level back deck, you can enjoy al - fresco meals at the dining table, as you take in the serenity of the private Zen Garden.

High - speed internet is available wired and wireless, along with smart TV with many popular web apps.

Guest access
Guests will enjoy exclusive use of the PalmTreeHouse apartment, one designated parking space, and the private deck and Zen Garden. Guests may also relax in the adjoining Koi Kabana.

Other things to note
The Koi Cabana is a new feature in the Zen Garden that will enhance your tranquility at the PalmTreeHouse! Your hosts will feed the fish each morning, and we can show you how to feed them, if you request it... however, we ask that you not place any human food or other objects, including your bodies, in the pond.

listing_name: 112 MARKET STREET RETREAT Down by the Riverwalk!
description:
Soaring ceilings. Exposed brick. Amazing charm! Welcome to 112 Market Street Retreat! History you can actually feel. This stunning 1BR condo in the historic Ahrens Building (circa 1907) sits half a block from the Cape Fear Riverwalk in the heart of Historic Downtown Wilmington. Walk to the best restaurants, breweries and rooftop views in the city. Boutique hotel vibes, without the lobby.

The space
Walk in and feel the vibe immediatley! This beautiful 1BR condo is located in the Ahrens Building circa 1907. It's in the heart of Historic Downtown Wilmington in a Neo-classical Revival style building. This is your perfect location a 1/2 block away from the river. You are in walking distance to pubs, cafes, restaurants, spas, art galleries, shopping and the beautiful Riverwalk. 1/2 block away is also the Level 5 Building that houses the Level 5 Rooftop bar & City Stage Theater. I recommend taking in the gorgeous sunsets while sipping on a glass of wine at one of the most scenic location in Wilmington! Hands down, you will enjoy the best view and venue in the region. If you enjoy local breweries, also a 1/2 block away is our neighbor The Front Street Brewery. Excellent food, local brews and service! If you would like something more upscale, dine at Circa 1922 with elegant cherry wood furnishings and the largest tapas and wine menu in town. Whether you are coming for business or pleasure, we have the perfect condo for all your needs. While walking around our historic town, you are sure to see our crews filming famous movies, mini series and commercials. You never know when you will run into stars who are continually filming in our Historic downtown locations. You can take a romantic carriage ride, eat at our many waterfront restaurants, take a ghost pub crawl tour, take a river tour, taxi to the Battleship North Carolina, or simply stroll the streets and enjoy the incredible atmosphere. If you are coming to Wilmington to enjoy dinner, theater, shopping, pubs and relaxing this elegant condo is the perfect fit. If you are coming to Wilmington to work, this is the perfect location to settle into and be close to everything downtown has to offer.

The renovated condo boasts high ceilings with beams, exposed brick through out, original tall windows with gorgeous wood shutters, original hardwood floors, bathroom with custom tile and fixtures. If you want cozy, urban and elegant, this is your condo!

We look forward to having you stay with us on your next visit to Historic Downtown Wilmington!

Amenities
Condo Features:
*1 BR, 1 Full bath, living room, kitchen
* Brand new Queen bed and mattress
*Original hardwoods through out
*Large original windows in living rm overlooks downtown
*Granite counter tops in kitchen & bathroom
*Central Heat & Air-Conditioning
*Flat Screen TV in LR & BR - Roku ready and live stream station
*Full Kitchen : Refrigerator, Stove top, convection oven/microwave, Pots, Pans, Keurig coffee maker and K-Cups, glassware, etc.

description:
We have a resident manager that lives on the property with a separate entrance, and is completely unseen and unheard throughout your stay. Enjoy this huge family home equipped with everything you need. 5 bedrooms, 3.5 bathrooms, fully equipped Chef's kitchen with new appliances, all linen and towels.

The space
This is a beautiful historic home on a special, family friendly street in downtown Wilmington. The house has exceptionally large, open rooms with many windows and light. There are 2 staircases-one in the front of the house, and one on the back of the house- so several family members can stay together and also maintain privacy in this large space. There are many architectural details that are left over from the 1920's, including the tile work in the bathrooms, the detailed carvings in the fireplace mantels, and the columns on the front porch of this southern colonial home.

Guest access

description:
Fish from the Dock!

Beautiful home with 110 feet on sandy beach on the ICWW on a huge lot. You are renting the entire downstairs with a screened in porch,. Private beach , dock and gorgeous views. There is a porch swing on the covered deck and a fenced yard.

The space
This location is like stepping back in time. You can walk out your front door, down a rustic wooden path through a natural area to a white sandy beach. You might see a neighbor but you are just as likely not to. And if you do see a neighbor be sure to wave hello. They'll return the greeting

Guest access
The guests will have access to the entire apartment. There are 2 bedrooms each with a Queen size bed. There is a full kitchen with a gas range, Dishwasher, Microwave and refrigerator with an icemaker. There is also a washer and dryer. The screened porch is yours alone to use with a swing and rocking chairs. The private beach has a dock, a tiki hut, beach chairs and an outdoor shower. The yard is totally fenced. we have 2 kayaks and 2 paddle boards you may use.
We require a minimum 3 day stay .

Other things to note
The house is on the Intracoastal Waterway but you see Masonboro Island from the beach to the right. It is a barrier island separating the ICWW from the ocean. It is an easy kayak paddle to get to the island and walk the nearly deserted beach for shells. I have a kayak you are welcome to use. The ICWW is like a road with most of the traffic in the middle where the deep channel is. The sides of the channel are like the shoulder of the road and are the best place to kayak. It is the safest as you are not competing with faster, bigger boats. You also need to be aware of the tides. Paddling against the current when you leave means you can ride the tide back with less effort.
We are pet friendly but have a few rules. If you, the one booking the reservation, would like to bring a pet they must be up to date on vaccinations ( vet records are required before checking in.) Tell me a little about the pet when you request the reservation, age, size (although i don't have a weight limit) and temperament. You know your pet.
If they do not get along with other dogs, bark excessively, chew on things or are a nuisance to the neighbors, etc. please make other arrangements.
Of course, you assume all responsibility for any damage caused by your pet.
Because of this, the person booking the reservation is the only one allowed to bring a pet.

description:
Nature’s Escape Guesthouse offers a private and peaceful retreat on a quiet three-acre wooded property with a beautiful spring-fed three-quarter-acre stocked pond. Conveniently located just seven miles from Wrightsville Beach and ten miles from downtown Wilmington, the guesthouse provides the perfect balance of seclusion and accessibility. Shopping, restaurants, and entertainment options, including a nearby movie theater, are also close by.

The space
The two-floor guesthouse is designed for comfort and convenience. The living area includes a cozy lounge with a 43-inch UHD Smart TV loaded with YouTubeTV, HBO MAX, Netflix, Disney+Hulu, and Amazon Prime. The fully equipped kitchen features a range, microwave, and full-size refrigerator with ice maker, along with everything you need to prepare your favorite meals and a coffee bar stocked with coffee, tea, powdered creamer, sweeteners, and snacks. A dining area offers a comfortable spot for meals or remote work.

Upstairs, you’ll find a bedroom with a California king-size bed, a 43-inch UHD Smart TV, and a dedicated desk workspace. The adjoining bathroom includes a walk-in shower. Guests also enjoy complimentary high-speed internet, dual climate control heating and air conditioning on both floors, plus an air purifier and dehumidifier for added comfort.

A highlight of recent renovations is the private screened-in porch, the perfect place to sip coffee in the morning, enjoy evening breezes, or simply relax in the fresh air while being sheltered from pests.

Guest access
Guests are invited to enjoy the vibrant natural setting, complete with wooded walking trails, lush native landscaping with azaleas, gardenias, ferns, and Japanese maples, and the spring-fed pond stocked with fish. Local birds, turtles, frogs, and even the occasional barn owl make the property feel like a private wildlife preserve. Shared outdoor spaces also include a fire pit, though firewood is not guaranteed onsite. Laundry facilities are available on the shared porch behind the main building.

Other things to note
For insurance reasons, guests must be at least 21 years old to book, and children under 12 cannot be accommodated. We are pet-friendly, though breed restrictions apply to exclude dangerous animals.

description:
This one of a kind property is truly the way to experience historic Wilmington. The 1700 square foot suite is located in the upper floor of the Firehouse.

Located at in the Castle Street Antique & Arts district of downtown Wilmington. Walk to wonderful restaurant, galleries, coffee shops.

The space
This one of a kind property is truly the way to experience historic Wilmington. Note: this is a second story unit without an elevator; the unit is not handicapped accessible; you must be able to climb stairs to access the unit.

In April 1915, Engine Company 2 began operation at 5th and Castle Streets. Their new station housed six men, two horses, and a two-horse hose wagon. Designed by local architect James F. Gause, Jr., the Italianate structure also housed a bell relocated from the corner of 4th and Dock Streets. The bell sounded alarms until the last fire horse was retired in 1918. In 1956, Engine 2 relocated to the new Headquarters Station on Dock Street. Downstairs is our beautiful Station No. 2 venue and courtyard. There are no events held while you are renting the upstairs Firehouse Suite.

Once the bunk house to the firemen, this spacious second floor apartment with open floor plan, has recently undergone an outstanding renovation. The gracious apartment is 1709 square feet of light & luxury. Decorated by an acclaimed designer, with understated historic detail throughout, this unique and very large space combines comfort and privacy with a refreshingly hip sty
The unit offers 14 foot ceilings, hardwood floors, exposed brick walls, concrete counter-tops, stainless steel appliances along with elegant furnishings and finishes. Original art work hangs in every room, including most pieces by renown local artists.

The living room offers quality and luxury including a large flat screen television with DVD, WiFi, relaxing seating for your group and a dining area large enough to seat 10 comfortably.

Pets are welcome with a pet fee. We ask that they stay off of the furniture. (Those with severe animal allergies should consider a different rental.)

The Firehouse is ideally located at the gateway to the Castle Street District. Enjoy the Saturday Peddlers Market while perusing unique antique shops, vintage clothiers, retro furnishings, wine shops and art galleries.

Only a few blocks further you can enjoy the activity and excitement of historic downtown Wilmington after a quiet night’s sleep in the historic Castle Street Firehouse located 5 blocks from the waterfront. You can take a lovely stroll through the beautiful historic homes or jump on the District Trolley which will pick you up at the corner or 5th and Castle. The location is convenient to everything downtown Wilmington has to offer: Thalian Hall, The Brooklyn Arts Center, casual to fine dining, hip coffee houses, art galleries, excellent county library, horse drawn carriage rides, Shops of Old Wilmington or the Cotton Exchange, historic tours, Screen Gems Studio tours, Ghosts of Wilmington Tour, haunted pub crawls, Wilmington’s Farmer’s Market (April – December), the Azalea Festival (April), Riverfest (October) and the NC Battleship. PPD, Cape Fear Community College and the new convention center are within easy walking distance. You can experience the night life, then walk a couple of blocks back to the apartment and spend a safe and quiet evening in your beautifully furnished unit. If you have a car, there is an abundance of free on-street parking . No need to pay for parking.

Please keep in mind that absolutely no house parties are allowed in the property.

Guest access
The Firehouse Suite is the 1700 2 bedroom suite on the second floor.

Please note that is a Ring doorbell/camera at the single red door off of Castle St. If you have any problems entering the unit please Ring us at any time and we will respond immediately.

Other things to note
Great restaurants, Rx, just across the street and Castle Street Kitchen next to Terra Sol Yoga (old church building)… wonderful art galleries, antique shops, Wilmington Wine, Jesters for breakfast or lunch and Luna Cafe for morning coffee..

For your convince we have hired great people to keep the place cleaned and landscaped but be mindful that we live near the coast and occasionally small bugs make their way inside the house, if this bothers you, consider staying somewhere else.

description:
Discover the perfect blend of luxury and convenience at our beautiful Wilmington, NC home. Only 3 minutes away from downtown and 15 minutes from the beach, our central location places you right in the heart of the action, surrounded by fantastic the Cargo district, coffee shops, bars, restaurants, and shops.

Please note, we ask each guest to declare the exact number of guests and pets joining their stay, as we have a ring doorbell system to keep track. Please respect our house rules.

The space
Nestled in a historic Wilmington home from the early 1900s, this spacious two-level residence is the epitome of elegance and sophistication. The upstairs features three expansive bedrooms, including a twin, full, and queen-sized bed, as well as a stunning master bathroom complete with a shower and clawfoot bathtub.

On the lower level, guests will find a magnificent master bedroom connected to a second bathroom, a grand living room and dining area, and a fully-equipped kitchen with a charming patio room. Additionally, for your convenience, a washer and dryer are located in the pantry.

We understand that pets are an important part of the family, which is why we allow pets on a case-by-case basis. Please notify us in advance if you plan to bring your furry companion, and note that a non-refundable deposit of $50 per dog is required in cash upon arrival for short-term rentals. Thank you for choosing our home, and we look forward to making your stay unforgettable.

Guest access
You will have access to all parts of the home including the courtyard.

Other things to note
We kindly ask for a $50 non-refundable pet fee per pet as we must perform an extra deep cleaning in case future guests have pet allergies. Please note that this fee doubles for two pets at $100. The deposit must be paid in cash upon arrival.

We want to make it clear that as a coastal town, NC summers can bring waterbugs which resemble roaches. We take routine measures to prevent them, but they may occasionally appear due to the hot climate. Please rest assured that they are harmless and we do our best to eliminate them from the property.

We welcome children under five free of charge, but please note that this home is not child-proof and contains many antiques and breakable items.

Please note that this is a lived-in house, which we thoroughly clean and prepare for our guests. While we offer all necessary amenities, we kindly ask that you understand this is not a hotel experience. You may find condiments in the fridge which are at your disposal.

Lastly, we ask that you plan to check-in prior to 9 pm, as we do not allow late check-ins after this time. Thank you for your cooperation and understanding.

description:
Erik's tidal marsh waterfront property close to UNCW, Airlie Gardens, Intracoastal Waterway (ICW), Wrightsville Beach, US-17/Ocean-Highway, I-40 & 8 miles to ILM airport–available only when I'm in Costa Rica! Bike, fish, kayak, paddle, run, skateboard, walk to UNCW. Observe tidal events, watch animals & birds, chill, relax, go surf, practice yoga on deck, pier & grass! Optimal for nature loving couple or tight singles willing to deal with 1943 house. Good vibes included at no extra costs! :-)

The space
Beautiful natural waterfront tidal marsh property! View birds, sunrises and sunsets from the floating dock, deck, tea pot, fire pit or therapy room. Perfect for an individual, an intimate couple, and an occasional guest, this well-located single bedroom house can comfortably (but not perfectly) sleep a second couple on the sofa or pull out in the MediaRoom or Therapy Room.

Kitchen includes an electric stove/oven, refrigerator/freezer, microwave, farm sink, and hickory and ceramic countertops. It's pretty old and is not the best kitchen ever. Erik has never been much of a cook, but he made sure the fridge keeps beers cold. For the real cooks of the Earth, there are plenty of quality cooking pots and pans. The remodeled kitchen is now much better than when Erik started working on this old house back in 2002! You won't be too disappointed.

Relax in our now world famous \Therapy Room\" featuring the calming but actively wonderful view of the tidal Bradley Creek salt marsh. You will feel great in this natural space. Keep your eyes open. Plan on seeing more animals while in this room rather than clocks
photos_count: read and think in this room. When not outside and not sleeping
photo_urls:
this is where Erik spends most of his time. He needs the therapy!

The one and only one bedroom. A bit tight but you will enjoy the naturally lighted space with a great view of Bradley Creek right from the queen sized bed. Those seeking serious darkness or privacy may not prefer windows with great views. A female guest once requested curtains. Erik likes it best with wide open view of birds and the creek and dogs! If you are the type of person worried about privacy
cohost_ids:
then you'd probably never use the outdoor shower and should probably go somewhere else (unless you're ready for a change:-)! The bedroom includes a closet and dresser to hang your dresses and suits and store clothes and belongings.

Work seriously from a small but comfortable corner desk with a Steelcase Leap ergonomic chair
superhost: indoor and outdoor WIFI network with monitor
longitude: mouse
guests: and audio speakers that you can easily connect your PC or phone.  (When I'm in Costa Rica
bedrooms:
I miss this desk and Leap chair. Try getting a comfortable ergonomic chair in Costa Rica. It ain't as easy as an Airbnb click.) Be sure to look up your favorite word using the dictionary stand near the desk.

The Media Room holds a bookshelf
baths: and couch that opens up to fold-out double sized sofa bed. I keep a small selection of books like dictionaries
registration: maps & thesaurus handy. Not many books here but we've got plenty of birds and words and maps!  All \"monitors\" or screens are usually NOT connected to cable or broadcast TV but internet is available via the LG47 \"TV\" in the media room.  Web browsers available on the LG47TV or you can use your own device to drive the screen.  Either by Ethernet cable
amenities: HDMI
instant_book:
or WiFi. The TV supports widi and miracast to make the TV your PC monitor.

The Deck
professional_management: though not as new as the kitchen
min_nights: is one of the newer functional parts of Hooker Haven.  A friend seeing my surfboard rugs had the idea to inlay the surfboard in it!  To orient yourself
cancellation_policy: the pier points North!  A very fitting place to absorb sun
extra_guest_fee:
read books and hear and watch birds.

Dear potential Airbnb Hooker Haven Guest who may be a naturalist or scientist or bird watcher or any person interested in animal behavior. Please take this seriously from me: the Deck coupled with the TherapyRoom
single_fee_structure: make Hooker Haven into one of the best bird watching places on the planet!  You will probably hear the KingFisher before you see her.  And
rating_overall:
unlike most other great observation locations you don't need to leave! :-)

Enter the \"Mudroom\" from the Deck–or from the Therapy Room or Kitchen. Perhaps
rating_accuracy: you're like the old Buddy LongDog who occasionally morphed into Muddy LongDog.  You may have sand and mud–the special Bradley Creek kind of mud–all over you.  Please wash BEFORE entering the mudroom from outside. BUT
rating_checkin: to take care of occasionally muddy dogs when you are not present
rating_cleanliness: it is possible for muddy dogs to enter the MudRoom through the DogDoor.  If you are muddy or dirty or salty and it's warm enough you can take an outdoor shower and clean up before entering the mudroom.  If you are dirty and it's freezing
rating_location: a dog (or cat) may enter the mudroom through the \"dog door\" (remember that P-Kitty was a dog) and they may have just taken a swim in the creek and a walk in the mud. It's very possible. So
rating_value: sometimes you need to \"protect\" the rest of the house from a muddy dog. In that case
ttm_revenue: you can shut the door from the mudroom to the therapy room and the door from the mudroom to the kitchen. That keeps a muddy wet dog off of your clean floors and furniture but still gives them access to food and protection from the weather. It's also a laundry room and bodega of sorts with the step ladder
ttm_revenue_native:
and house tools.

The Fire Pit is to your left--the West--as you walk out a North facing door. This is a great spot for roasting marshmallows! You will find a bench swing with a beautiful view that is just perfect for two lovers. The swing is great with or without fire. Typically
ttm_avg_rate: I fill the fire pit with pine cones and branches from our numerous trees. If you seek a quick fire after a sunset
ttm_avg_rate_native:
the pine yard debris is fine. For a quality campfire or to cook food you need to step up from pine wood to something better suited to cooking--like oak. Be careful walking around the fire pit. Near the Fire Pit the yard is significantly slanted down towards the creek.

To the northeast of the Fire Pit
ttm_occupancy: please find our Ephemeral Spring. A freshwater spring that naturally flows out of the ground briefly during and following periods of significant local rainfall. Even though the spring is directly next to tidal salt water
ttm_adjusted_occupancy:
the freshwater keeps a few trees close to the saltwater well watered even if it does not appear to be ebbing out of the ground at a given moment. A few years back I built a bench out of granite rocks right next to the spring. It's a relaxing bench to view the creek and watch birds. Sometimes you will be lucky enough to hear the sounds of water ebbing out of the ground. Lots of birds and other animals know about the fresh water and show up more often when it is actively flowing. Your dog will figure it out too! The birds even take baths in it! Amazing to observe two different types of water come together.

Pointing to the North
ttm_revpar: the Pier & Floating Dock conveniently provide tidal access to the run of the creek. Dogs (and people) can jump off floating dock into the run of the creek. You can also launch kayaks
ttm_adjusted_revpar:
or other small water vehicles. Enjoy a close look at many types of marine life from the pier or floating dock. Send Erik your amazing pictures of animals!

Depart the pier South and head East to the Teapot Garden. Go all the way to the East and look at the shape of the stone retaining wall. See the Teapot?

From the cover of the Teapot take Lovers Lane to the South entrance of the Compass Patio. Notice the perimeter marked by pine trees and rocks surrounding a Tupelo tree (aka black cherry or black gum) in the exact center. Significant points of the compass are marked by triangular shaped rocks. The perimeter is also defined by Wisteria arbor that goes fully around the black cherry tree. When the wisteria vine blooms
ttm_adjusted_revpar_native: come here to see 360 degrees of beautiful purple wisteria flowers and be surrounded by the succulent scents. On a really hot day
ttm_avg_min_nights:
the noticeable difference in cooler temperature and vertical breezes of this patio are quite amazing when compared to the rest of the property.

Guest access
You will be happy with this one! You have quick access to UNCW and Wrightsville Beach via your car or bike or walking. You have easy access to Bradley Creek directly from our backyard and you can arrange to use our tandom kayak to paddle out as far as the Intracoastal Waterway
ttm_blocked_days: or the Atlantic Ocean (assuming you're strong and ready for a possibly cold
l90d_revenue: tough and wet paddle). Airlie Gardens is 1 mile down the road and offers a great opportunity to tour
l90d_revenue_native: attend a concert or wedding and view more than a century of southern gardens by the sea and lots of birds! You can even paddle to Airlie Gardens in our kayaks. For an easier paddle
l90d_avg_rate: plan to depart our dock on the outgoing tide
l90d_avg_rate_native:
and then catch the incoming tide for an easy paddle back home:-)

Other things to note
Yes! You can bring your traveler friends and family as long as you FULLY UNDERSTAND the place is best suited for a couple or a single. Great Airbnb reviews depend on us meeting and exceeding your expectations. So
l90d_occupancy: if you don't fully understand this and come here with another couple or more people
l90d_adjusted_occupancy: it will be difficult for us to meet your expectations.  It is certainly possible for two couples–we've done it a lot–but if you're the type that needs an hour alone in the bathroom
l90d_revpar_native:
we have an awesome outdoor shower that makes it easier for multiple people to be here. The outdoor shower is cool and has such a great view that Erik never showers indoors if the temperature is above freezing! If you're coming back from surfing in cold water it's always better to get that wetsuit off under hot water in the outdoor shower!

Less is better. There is ONLY ONE bedroom. Though it is NOT our goal
l90d_adjusted_revpar: we can comfortably and technically sleep five with one couple in queen bed in the bedroom
l90d_adjusted_revpar_native: another couple in the full size pull out sofa in the media room
l90d_avg_min_nights: and another person on couch in therapy room.  If it is not too cold
l90d_avg_length_of_stay: two can shower outside and two can shower inside at the same time!  Provide us with your total number of guests so that we are aware of your needs for bed linens and bath towels.  Be aware
l90d_reserved_days: this place is not perfect for those who crave privacy or like locking doors."

description:
Centrally located, pet friendly cottage near the Cargo District with easy keyless entry and 2 on street parking spots directly in front.

2 dogs under 30 lbs each; $30 per pet per stay.

You have access to the whole home besides our owners closet where we store all supplies. Please keep in mind that Wilmington is a diverse city with a lot of growth happening, which is what makes it so special. Can’t wait to welcome you and provide you with any other info you need for an amazing trip!

The space
Front porch - bistro set and glider
Living room - Wi-Fi, smart tv, lamp with charging stations, air mattress provided
Dining room - table that can be used as desk, wooden dining table to eat/play games at, 6 chairs provided
Kitchen - coffee maker, toaster, microwave, oven/stove, fridge with ice maker. All basic utensils, dinnerware, pots and pans
Bathroom - shower/tub, hair dryer, fresh towels provided
Bedroom 1&2 - queen size hybrid mattresses, fresh sheets and blanket provided, pillows, dresser, one full length mirror, closet space, bedside lamps with charging stations
Laundry room - washer and dryer with soap and dryer sheets

Guest access
Entire home except one locked owners room we keep all supplies for the property in.

Other things to note
We exterminate and treat the inside and outside of the living area on a routine basis. Due to the tropical climate ( Temperature, humidity, rain), bugs are endemic and you may encounter them during your stay. In an effort to help with critters, we ask that you do not leave food out. If you do see any bugs, please notify us immediately.

listing_name: Artsy Studio - Walk Everywhere!  Historic Downtown
description:
Great central city location! Comes with parking spot!- a rarity in central downtown! Cozy, eclectic studio with tin ceilings, decorated with an artists touch in the 1914 Historic McClellan Livery building in the absolute best location downtown! Secure and gated building in old New Orleans style. Explore everything Historic downtown has to offer and never get in your car! We're just moments by foot from the historic Riverwalk, great restaurants, shops, museums, breweries, and nightlife.

The space
Located in a former livery (carriage stable) constructed in 1914, this unique 350 square foot studio is now part of a New Orleans style building. The space has high ceilings with beautiful ornamental pressed tin, unique lighting and decor in the style of Old Paris. The full kitchen has stainless appliances and a granite bar-countertop. The open living space has a modern sofa, comfortable pillow top queen bed (linens provided), a flat panel TV with Roku and free Wifi. There's a coin op laundry room on premise as well. You're right in the middle of bustling historic downtown! Comes with FREE parking, a rarity in central downtown! Plus... You get use of the private outdoor common patio. Completely private in the middle of the city!

Guest access
Guests will have full access to the studio via gated entry. Guests are also able to use the laundry facility, private patio area and the outdoor reading nook on the upper level of the building.

Other things to note
If you need some staples, the Village Market is right across the street. We provide a parking space for you as well - a rarity in central downtown since most all parking is paid. Instructions are provided to confirmed guests. The area is generally safe but our parking lot is not gated so we do not recommend leaving bikes, surfboards, valuables or any easily accessible items on or in your vehicle in the lot.

Noise in the unit: Although it's typically pretty quiet, our space has a through the wall HVAC unit and a dehumidifier which cause some noise as they cycle on and off. If you're a very light sleeper, you may wish to bring earplugs or noise canceling headphones. Of course, you can simply turn the units off. Downtown can be quite lively on weekends and during festivals with multiple breweries and night spots so you may hear music close by.

Location note: We've lived in Wilmington for a long time and find it quite safe downtown. Our building is gated, but keep in mind that downtown's popularity results a lot of visitors and sometimes folks overdo it in the bars, or others might be wandering around late at night after the venues close. If this makes you uneasy despite our building being gated, you may wish to find accommodation further from downtown since our location is right in the middle of things.

We provide linens and towels and there are typically a few extras in the walk in closet.

Six legged critters: I hate talking about this, but I must. Palmetto bugs are indigenous to our area and they are extremely prolific downtown since we are close to the river. They look like giant roaches and they're a fact of life here. They're quick and sneaky but don't want to be indoors any more than you want to see them. Despite having pest control, it is rare but possible you will see one inside, especially since we're on the ground floor and they're practically impossible to keep completely out if there's a door that opens. They're high on the ick factor but completely harmless and can be chased out or otherwise dispatched.

WiFi: Though our WiFi is generally fast and reliable, we cannot guarantee speed or availability if there are issues on the provider's end of things or outside of our control. If WiFi is absolutely essential, we recommend you have hotspot or other alternative capabilities just in case.

description:
Solidly renovated 1866 Italianate House in the Historic District in the Downtown Wilmington neighborhood .Original old hardwood floors, footstep-worn thresholds, tiled fireplaces & time-shifted glass panes tell you this house has been here 150 plus years ! Our 3 bedroom/2-bath/full-kitchen duplex is walking distance to anywhere downtown & a short 17 minute drive to the beach ! A very large backyard for hanging out and BBQ shared with owner. We are pet friendly ; fee is $ 65 per pet per stay.

The space
Hello GUEST
We expect you around 5-6 PM but if this is not correct and you come later , let us know before your arrival. We live next door with our 9 year old who goes to sleep at 8 PM , which means we will be upstairs and won't hear any knocking . We live on N.6th Street near the Princess St. intersection and the house is colored pale rose. Your entrance is on the left and when you arrive just knock on the door on the right .The Doorknob in front is historic and decorative, please use Metal handle to push door open and be gentle with the old doors.
Now about the place.....You have a functioning kitchen with glasses and cups for all beverages, a coffee machine with filters and grinder plus toaster and a working oven . Recycling and trash pickup is early on Monday morning so we will let you know when we pull all the receptacles to the street . Let us know if you need another bag .Please put all TRASH out on sunday evening
Linens and towels are all there with soap but you will need Shampoo if ours is wrong for you, shower gel and more toilet paper.
The 2 toilets use Eco-Flush so please hold handle 3 seconds when you flush.
You have 2 bedrooms upstairs , both with queen beds plus a sunroom in the back with a doublebed . The Sunroom is accessible through the second bedroom but all the rooms have doors for privacy. There is a Shower and bathroom upstairs. There is also a fully equipped laundryroom. Laundry detergent is ultra concentrated so please fill cap up to line 2 for each load. When using dryer, please put on LOW HEAT, HIGH does not work .Extra blankets are in each room. Access to upper floor is by spiral staircase from living room and by a straight stairwell from back porch leading to second bedroom upstairs. Downstairs is a queen sofa bed with the sheets and pillows in the grey ottoman, a bathroom with tub and shower.
Any other questions please do not hesitate to contact us ......a lot to read uh.
Amy and Luca

Guest access
We enjoy sitting out on our front porch and in our back yard and want you to do so too! We'll make sure you have your place to enjoy being outside and have access to a BBQ grill.

Other things to note
Since AirBnb has no pet fee, we add it when notified by guests. It is $ 65 per pet per stay.

listing_name: The Royal - Downtown Wilmington, Walk to All!
description:
Introducing The Royal: Wilmington’s Regal Rental Experience. Just steps away from the Port City’s premium wedding destinations and concert venues, The Royal is able to comfortably accommodate up to 12 guests with five king size beds and one perfectly situated queen.

The space
The Princess Kate (bottom floor space)

The Princess Kate comfortably accommodates up to 4 guests with two king size beds and 1.5 baths. With one bedroom located off the living room and the other nestled off the back hallway, guests are able to spread out and find their own space. The shower/tub combination is located off the primary bedroom. A coffee maker, microwave, stove/oven combo, and full refrigerator are able to accommodate everything you may need during your stay. Two back doors open to the shared fenced common space.

The Queen Elizabeth (top floor space - accessible by stairs only)

The Queen Elizabeth is the larger of the two units, sleeping up to 8 guests, with 3 king size beds, one queen and 2 full bathrooms (showers only, tub in primary bath decorative only). description:
When staying at the Carriage House, the beach and Wilmingtons' attractions are yours for the taking. The Carriage House is located in the Princess Place Neighborhood, next to Burnt Mill Creek -a bird watchers paradise. It is 1.5 miles to Downtown Wilmington and the Riverwalk and 7 miles to the Beach. I have crafted the Carriage House from reclaimed materials. Enjoy the guest hot tub and fire table. Snowbirds and travel nomads know that Wilmington is wonderful year round!

The space
The Carriage house is a whimsical expression of the host, using reclaimed and creatively re-purposed materials and family treasures. There is a comfortable king size bed in the bedroom. The sofa in the living room will fold into a twin bed if you are bringing a pre-approved child. Essentially, you have a separate one bedroom apartment with a kitchen-and open living area with a back deck and a screened front porch. The backyard offers a fire table for cool evening enjoyment and a hot tub dedicated for guest use in a serene garden area outside the carriage house.
The Carriage House is ideal for a couple or for two friends visiting Wilmington. I do allow extra occupancy for one child (18 years or younger) accompanying a parental unit. If you request an extra adult guest and if I grant your request, there will be a $75 per night add on fee.

Guest access
You will have exclusive access to a screened front porch and canopy covered back deck. The washer and dryer in the main house are available for your usage, if you need to refresh your towels after visiting the beach or would like a load a laundry done. I am happy to do this for you at no charge. The dedicated 4 man hot tub is reserved for the Carriage House and Casita guests only. The Casita is the apartment below the Carriage House. The fire table is for roasting marshmallows and enjoying the crisp Fall and Winter weather (Spring and Summer too of course) Please enjoy my yard. In addition to your deck and screened porch at the Carriage House, I have a large deck and sitting areas in the back yard. I have perennials and other flowers in the gardens. The Celeste fig tree's produces delicate and tasty figs in July. The herb garden is open to you to enhance your culinary pursuits. I enjoy and grow tropical flowers. And I have cats and a silly little dog, Gomez, that you are free to pet and play with, if you are so inclined.

Other things to note
I am LGBT and Business Traveler friendly. While the number of guests is set at two (2), I do allow for a third occupant, if the person is a child (18 or younger) and I have been advised. I have a Ring door bell at my private home at the front door and a Ring security camera the back porch of my private home.

listing_name: 1914 Stable Skylit Urban Studio Historic Downtown
description:
One block from RiverWalk! Comes with parking space - a rarity in central downtown! Unique industrial, eclectic studio with barrel ceilings and a skylight in the Historic McClellan Livery building in the absolute best location downtown! Secure and gated building in old New Orleans style. Explore everything Historic downtown has to offer and never get in your car! You're just steps from great restaurants, the Riverwalk, shops, museums, breweries and more!

The space
This super unique ca. 1914 space in a gated building has high barrel ceilings, a skylight and unique architectural features that make it a one-of-a-kind stay. The studio is about 350SF but has all the comforts of home including a full kitchen with granite counters and all dishes, pots and utensils. Space is heated and cooled and has a soft, inviting queen bed. Towels and linens are provided as well as some bath basics if you need them. Decor is urban, artistic, industrial...combining the unique tastes of your hosts. The best features? We provide a dedicated parking space behind the building...something nearly impossible to find in the center of downtown! Simply park your car and walk to absolutely everything downtown Wilmington has to offer!

Guest access
Guests can enjoy the entire studio and the common areas inside the gate. There's a relaxing area upstairs to sit and read or play games. The downstairs patio is a nice spot to enjoy a glass of wine or a snack... it's all private. Laundry facilities (coin op) are available for your use. Please use the parking space designated... It's free.

Other things to note
If you need some staples, the Village Market is right across the street. We provide a parking space for you as well - a rarity in central downtown. Instructions are provided to confirmed guests. The area is quite safe but our parking lot is not gated so we do not recommend leaving bikes, surfboards, valuables or any easily accessible items on or in your vehicle.

Noise in the unit: Our space has a through the wall HVAC unit and a dehumidifier which cause some noise as they cycle on and off. If you're a very light sleeper, you may wish to bring earplugs or noise canceling headphones.... or you can simply turn the units off. You may also hear the rear door to the building closing in the courtyard. Downtown can be quite lively on weekends and during festivals with multiple breweries and night spots so you may hear music close by.

Location note: We've lived in Wilmington for a long time and find it quite safe downtown. Our building is gated, but keep in mind that downtown's popularity results a lot of visitors and sometimes folks overdo it in the bars, or others might be wandering around late at night after the venues close. If this makes you uneasy despite our building being gated, you may wish to find accommodation further from downtown since our location is right in the middle of things.

We provide linens and towels and there are typically a few extras in the walk in closet.

We have a darkening curtain hidden behind the window curtains if light from the courtyard is bothersome. Additionally, if morning light from the skylight will interfere with sleep, you may wish to bring a sleep mask.

Six legged critters: I hate talking about this, but I must. Palmetto bugs are indigenous to our area and they are extremely prolific downtown since we are close to the river. They look like giant roaches and they're a fact of life here. They're quick and sneaky but don't want to be indoors any more than you want to see them. Despite having pest control, it is rare but possible you will see one inside, especially since we're on the ground floor and they're practically impossible to keep completely out if there's a door that opens. They're high on the ick factor but completely harmless and can be chased out or otherwise dispatched.

WiFi: Though our WiFi is generally fast and reliable, we cannot guarantee speed or availability if there are issues on the provider's end of things or outside of our control. If WiFi is absolutely essential, we recommend you have hotspot or other alternative capabilities just in case.

listing_name: Beautiful, charming, near riverfront and the beach
description:
Location, location, location!!! We are conveniently located just off Market Street, in beautiful Forest Hills. Just 2 miles to Historic Downtown Wilmington and it's beautiful waterfront area filled with shops and dining establishments, and 6 miles to the beautiful white sand of Wrightsville Beach, one of the most beautiful beaches on the east coast. We are also a mere 2 miles from Wilmington International Airport.

The space
As we find ourselves in challenging times, trying to get back to some kind of normal, we want to assure our guests that we are making every possible effort to ensure their comfort and safety when they stay at one of our properties. We want you to know that we use a professional cleaning service that strictly follows the most state-of-the-art procedures when it comes to disinfection. The disinfectant that they use is EPA registered and CDC approved. Their teams are trained to comply with OSHA standards and have completed a Covid-19 awareness certification course. The health and safety of our guests is their greatest priority.

Additionally, we have keyless entry keypad locks so you can self check-in.

In addition to being crazy clean… our property is also gorgeous and comfortable! The house was remodeled completely in 2016 and because of Hurricane Florence, was remodeled again in 2018! During that remodel, we updated the 2nd bathroom to include a beautiful, subway tiled, walk-in shower with frameless double glass doors, a new marble backsplash in the kitchen and added wonderful deep seat wicker furniture to our deck, to which we’ve just added gorgeous new cushions! We’ve also added new mattresses throughout the home and now have queens in both the master and 2nd bedroom, twin beds in the 3rd bedroom and a queen sleeper sofa in the living room, easily accommodating 8 guests… or more with the inflatable mattresses that we’ve added.
Our split bedroom design is perfect for providing privacy as the master bedroom is at the opposite end of the house from the other two bedrooms.

Our beautiful, bright eat-in kitchen is equipped with everything you could possibly need to feel right at home…Keurig and Mr. Coffee coffee makers, a stand mixer, electric griddle, crockpot, toaster, electric kettle and more!! We have a 49 inch smart TV, Roku to stream Netflix, Hulu, Amazon Prime, etc. and an antenna which gives you all the major networks, a BlueRay player and hundreds of DVD’s

Lastly, but probably the most important to many people is our location…it just can’t be beat for convenience… just about 2 miles to Historic Downtown Wilmington and the bustling nightlife of the riverfront and about 6 miles to the sandy white beaches of beautiful Wrightsville Beach. We’re also just a five minute drive to Wilmington International Airport.

Guest access
The entire house is yours!

Other things to note
We are just minutes to the airport, Historic Downtown Wilmington and The Cape Fear Riverfront, the shopping malls and Wrightsville Beach.

description:
Waterfront with pier, dock, our home and grounds are nestled on a secluded salt marsh tidal creek between the Intracoastal Waterway and Run of Little Creek tidal creek and across from Eagle Point Golf Club. Enjoy kayaking, fishing and crabbing for fun. Bicycle and walk on our quiet, dead-end street. Privacy and nature, yet with nearby beaches, restaurants, shopping, golf, boating opportunities. Nearby is the historic city of Wilmington on the Cape Fear River. Parties and events are not allowed.

The space
Beautiful beaches at the coastal town of Wrightsville Beach are only 15 -25 minutes (depending on traffic) where there is also a branch of the New Hanover County Public Library, a health food store, kayak/paddleboard/watersport and equipment rentals, and spectacular waterfront dining. The colonial city of Wilmington and its historic district and riverfront park is only 14 miles. Other area attractions include Airlie Gardens, the North Carolina Aquarium, the Boardwalk at Carolina Beach, historic Fort Fisher, and the Battleship North Carolina. There are numerous golf courses in the area. Or just chill in our rope hammock!
Most of the neighbors are permanent residents. Bald Eagle Lane dead ends with only a handful of homes beyond ours, and so there is very little traffic, certainly no through traffic, making it ideal for bicycling and walking. From our front porch and street view between the single row of waterfront homes to the Intercoastal Waterway and the vast salt marsh across which sits the oceanfront private barrier island of Figure Eight Island (gated community). The view from the back of the house is across the tidal creek to the other shore lined with century-old live oaks and the private, exclusive golf course Eagle Point adding to the green and natural vistas.
Enjoy birdwatching for osprey, herons (great grey, white, green), kingfishers, cardinals, and migrating waterfowl. To support local wildlife and birds, we are transitioning to native plants and a more natural environment, so you may find our grounds less formal than our neighbors.From our private pier and dock we enjoy, fishing and crabbing (for fun, not dining), launching our kayaks for a paddle out to the bay or Intracoastal Waterway and across to the Figure Eight Island salt marsh creeks, viewing the sunset and tidal shifts. With the daily changing tide, every 24 hours the creek water level swings through a spectacular scenic high tide, at times nearly to pier level, through a \dead\" low tide exposing areas of the creek bottom and its breeding ground for schools of minnows. There is a channel running in front of the pier
listing_type: so even at low tide a kayak can often be launched
room_type: the creek may not be navigable at dead low times depending on the time of year/tide swings. We have three sit-on-top kayaks available for your use: one two-person kayak; two one one-person kayaks. Walking or swimming in the creek is not allowed due to sharp oyster shells and unstable mud creek bottom. While Wilmington has some alligators in fresh water lakes to the south of us

listing_name: Stunning Sunsets, Walkable Condo, Covered Parking
description:
Bright, clean, comfortable apartment on the Cape Fear Riverwalk with exceptional views and sunsets. Lovingly decorated with custom furniture and a warm, personal touch.
Your own dedicated covered parking space is seconds away across the street. Enjoy walking to Wilmington’s vibrant restaurant scene and downtown wedding venues!
6 minutes from the Convention Center, 10-15 minutes from the airport (ILM) and only 25 minutes from Wrightsville Beach. Relax and enjoy life on the River!

The space
Custom decorations with a personal touch and nautical theme, including Audubon prints of birds you'll see from the balcony and a custom driftwood end table in the living room.

Small, well-equipped kitchen (a chef stayed here!)
Coffee & tea provided.
Comfortable Queen Bed with EuroTop mattress
Electronic blinds and room-darkening curtains
Couch if needed for sleeping a third person (linens for the couch are in the drawers under the TV)
Covered Parking

From a recent guest's message:

“If you are staying in Wilmington this is the place to be!. The location is in the middle of everything. You can park the car and forget it. The condo in right on the river walk next to the boat tours. There are several restaurants and bars within a few blocks. The organization of the condo was fantastic. Bob has everything laid out for you. As soon as you walk in there are folders hanging from the wall showing what there is to do, how to work the tv, where to take the trash and a large blue folder on the table with tons of info on restaurants and attractions. Far and away the most organized place we have stayed. Bob was extremely responsive and very helpful. There are also at least 3 beaches within a 20-30 minute drive. We really enjoyed Kure Beach. Overall. We would go back in a heartbeat.”

Nearby beaches include Wrightsville and Carolina Beaches (25 minutes) and Kure (30 minutes ).

Guest access
Enjoy the entire condo during your stay!

Other things to note
Two-night minimum stay.

description:
Inspiration awaits at the BahrHaus International Art Museum Studio + Loft. Guests are invited to enjoy the creativity and comfort of the entire 3rd floor at a Historic Southern Mansion turned private Art Museum. A haven for art enthusiasts, the loft is beaming with natural light and houses a rotating exhibit of selected works by artist Bahr III and a Private family Collection. Discover Downtown Wilmington just a short 5 block walk away or be at the beach by car in less than 15 mins.

The space
Enjoy the comfort of our entire finished attic space (1500 sq ft.) outfitted with a king size bed and private bathroom as you peruse a selection of rotating curated artworks from our private collection. Situated in the heart of historic downtown Wilmington, the property boasts ample porch space to sip a beverage, read a book, and settle into the relaxed southern living at any time of the day. Enjoy a scenic 5 block walk past impressive architecture to our top voted Riverwalk along the Cape Fear and immerse yourself in the cuisine offerings, attractions, and all that Historic Downtown Wilmington has to offer. Property includes private parking within the gated compound, a lift for your luggage, privacy of a dedicated floor and should you want some company, we are happy to share a coffee, a meal, and point you in the right direction to have the best experience possible.

Guest access
This AirBnb includes the entire finished attic loft space with private bathroom (1500 sq ft) on the top floor of the mansion. It is accessible by the back staircase (it is on the 3rd floor, so be aware if stairs are an issue). You will be given your own access code for the electronic lock on the back porch by the stairs and your luggage can be placed in the lift so you don't have to carry it up. We may invite you on a tour of other parts of the house, however we do ask that you refrain from exploring other areas of the house alone without a host. Private driveway to access the property with a car is at a gated entrance along 6th St. but you are welcome to park in front of the house as well.

description:
This is a recently renovated rustic 1947 cottage with an outstanding view of and access to the Intracoastal Waterway. It is perfect for meditation or quiet time by the water's edge, or for fishing, art, reading, writing, kayaking or paddleboarding. It is close to Wilmington, Wrightsville Beach, Topsail Island and Hampstead where there is shopping, restaurants, outdoor and cultural activities and beautiful beaches. It's old, but it is full of charm!

The space
This is a cozy cottage with a BIG view of the Intracoastal Waterway. Wake up to dolphins swimming by or stay up for the Moonrise over the water. This home offers peace and tranquility, but is just a short drive to all that Wilmington and Wrightsville Beach has to offer -- restaurants, shopping, nightlife and beaches along the Atlantic Ocean. The cottage has two bedrooms, each with a queen bed, and there is a modern bathroom with shower. It has a small kitchen and nice living spaces. We offer free wi-fi and a smart TV to our guests. There are books to read, puzzles to put together and cards to play. Be sure to bring a fishing rod, kayak or canoe!

Guest access
This is a small single-family cottage and guests have access to the whole home. Laundry facilities are available to those planning for long-term stays only. Guests are welcome to throw a fishing line in to catch a flounder or other fish from the dock. Swimming is not recommended as there are oyster beds in the area. The dock can also be used for launching paddle boards and/or kayaks. Kayaks are available for use by our guests upon request. We keep our own boat at the dock and in warm months, will be using the boat but will do our very best so as not to disturb our guests.

Other things to note
Please park in designated parking area on the left side of the driveway only. Do not block driveway as it is a shared driveway. Park on the left side of the driveway only.

This cottage is not suitable for children under the age of 8. It has a high deck and is waterfront. It also has soft pine floors and is not suitable for crawling infants. In addition, there is possible lead paint underneath other layers of paint. We love children, but I don’t recommend this home for young children.

There is an information sheet posted on the refrigerator door that tells our guests everything they need to know including wifi information and where the trash cans are located outside.

Please no tampons or other feminine products in the toilet as we are on a septic system. Also, use of hair dye or hair dye or bleach products is prohibited inside the house. These products cause stains that can not be removed and you wouldn't believe how many women want to color their hair while on vacation!

*** Pet Policy ***
No Pets are allowed. The floors are soft pine and their nails damage them. We love our little four-legged friends but our floor doesn't. So sorry!

description:
Your perfect wedding group or large family spot!

Make yourself and large group at home in this Historic home with 2400 sqft of comfortable living. There is plenty of space for up to 12 people. This unit is the 2nd and 3rd floor of the home.

The front steps do not have handrails and may not suit those with balance or other instability issues.

The space
Your stay puts you right in the middle of everything going on in downtown Wilmington. You will be staying a block and a half from the Wilson performing arts center, one block from the Brooklyn Arts center, 4 blocks from the north end of the Brooklyn arts District, 3 blocks from the cotton exchange, and 5 blocks from the Market and Water Street intersection. Wrightsville beach is a short 20 min uber or lyft ride, along with Carolina Beach being a 30 min drive. The home is close to restaurants, dining, and all the downtown nightlife.

If you require 12 or more guests please or would like to spread out more use this link to see the separate (not included) 1st floor unit.

https://www.airbnb.com/rooms/36598370

Both units have private entrances, kitchens, etc and “do not” share any indoor space.

The units can both be rented by booking each listing for a total of 18 guests (12 and 6)

Guest access
In order to receive access and In compliance with HOA guidelines the booker must provide a valid photo ID and phone number. Failure to provide the requested information two weeks prior to checkin and immediately upon booking if within 14 days of the stay, Is cause for cancelation of the reservation without refund.

Other things to note
HA rules do not allow for the property to be rented to guests under 26 years of age or guests under 26 that are not family.

The home is a historic property that was divided into an upstairs (this unit 2nd and 3rd floor) and a downstairs unit. The units have separate entrances and only share the front porch and backyard (less patio) as common areas. Parking is located in the backyard at the rear of the property, this unit gets 4 spaces two deep one vehicle in front of the other. This leaves two spots for the downstairs guests. The property is monitored with a security system.

If bringing pets I must be notified and the animals must be approved prior to arrival. No cats or dogs over 40lbs

There is a food bank that operates in the block 2 times a week. It does make driving on the street difficult during the operating period. However it does not affect getting to the parking area behind the house at all.

*** Please download the Airbnb app and use that as the primary method of communication, however my number is available in case of emergency after calling emergency services. when calling call the number back to back to bypass the do not disturb setting on the phone that may be on.***

description:
Bright/cheerful cottage 8 miles from Wrightsville Beach and 4 miles from downtown Wilmington and UNCW. This small cottage offers 750 square feet of comfort and convenience in a quiet tree-lined neighborhood. Private driveway provides off street parking. The interior offers private bedrooms, kitchen, and washer/dryer. The cottage is attached to the owners' house by an open breezeway-no shared walls. Even though the owners live on site full time, you can still have your own secluded get away.

The space
Perfect for a family of four or two couples. If you want a quiet and very comfortable place to escape to - this is it. The cottage has its own driveway and private access. There are no shared walls with the owner’s main house. It offers 9 foot ceilings and includes two bedrooms (1 queen, 2 standard twins; each bedroom offers USB chargers), full bath, fully stocked kitchen, and comfortable living room. The newly renovated kitchen includes full size refrigerator, oven/stove, microwave and Keurig coffee pot. There is not a dishwasher or ice machine.

Guest access
The cottage has everything you will need. Private parking and your own private entrance. The front door has a keyless entry - keypad deadbolt. Let us know when you will arrive and Airbnb will give you the code. There is a grassy area out front where you can relax in the shade and enjoy the fresh air. There is a long, deep seated bench under the dogwood tree outside the front door where you can unwind after a full day at the beach or enjoy a cocktail before heading downtown to any of Wilmington’s fabulous restaurants.

Other things to note
This property only holds the cottage and the owner’s main house. You will have your own private space and parking. The owners park in front of the main house and on the opposite side of the backyard. Guests are not allowed in the back yard, there is ample green space at the front of the cottage. Owners can provide extra outdoor chairs, if needed. This cottage is centrally located and has quick and easy access to shopping, downtown Wilmington, UNCW, and the Beach.

description:
Welcome to the SeaHob. Total upstairs privacy. Seahob has two bedrooms that is just perfect after a long day enjoying the beach . Relax in a private and peaceful neighborhood away from all the hustle and bustle but close enough to your favorite restaurants. Easy 3 minute drive to Carolina Beach and a 5 minute drive to Kure Beach . Keep in mind we will be sharing our home with you . This is not suitable for children under the age of 12 . Please read our house manual and find us on fb @ SeaHob .

The space
Our upstairs contains two bedrooms with a large bathroom area. Each room has it's own TV with one room with a queen bed and the other room with a full bed. Downstairs has a big kitchen area that is shared space. Who's ready for a cocktail ?

Guest access
